microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN no microwave BECAUSE observed fruit meals were explicitly served cold without reheating. IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND non-fruit foods without sauces or dips THEN 1 min BECAUSE an earlier personal savory meal used light reheating. IF working on a laptop or tablet AND neutral affect AND non-fruit foods without sauces or dips THEN 2 min to 3 min, with current evidence leaning toward 3 min when the screen is in front BECAUSE repeated working meals used more reheating than personal dining, and the new front-laptop savory meal also used the hottest setting. IF social setting with a person on the left AND neutral affect AND savory foods with a dip or sauce available THEN 2 min BECAUSE the observed left-social savory meal used stronger reheating. IF social setting with a person on the right AND neutral affect AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN no microwave BECAUSE the observed right-social fruit meal was served without reheating, reinforcing that fruit is preferred cold even in social dining. IF watching TV AND neutral affect AND savory foods with a dip or sauce available THEN 3 min BECAUSE observed TV meals used the strongest reheating.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF social setting with a person on the left AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE the user wanted to keep the left-side partner visible. IF social setting with a person on the right AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the mirrored social meal showed the same side-specific view-preservation preference. IF watching TV with TV in front AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the user chose to preserve the front view for media. IF working on a laptop or tablet with laptop in front AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the new front-laptop meal shows the same front-view preservation preference while working. IF watching TV with TV on the right AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE in the observed right-TV meal the user did not prioritize preserving the side view. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN mixed evidence: do not consider occlusion in one left-laptop meal and minimize left occlusion in another BECAUSE left-side work contexts do not yet show a stable rule. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the right AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN mixed evidence: minimize right occlusion in at least one meal, but do not consider occlusion in another BECAUSE right-side work contexts also do not produce a stable view-preservation preference. IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND foods without sauces or dips THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE no view-preservation priority was selected in personal dining.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN slow BECAUSE the user selected a comfort-oriented pace in personal fruit dining. IF social setting with a person on the left AND neutral affect AND savory foods with sauce available THEN slow BECAUSE the observed social meal used slow speed. IF social setting with a person on the right AND neutral affect AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN slow BECAUSE the mirrored social meal also used slow speed, reinforcing a comfort-oriented pace in interpersonal settings. IF watching TV with TV in front AND neutral affect AND savory foods with sauce available THEN slow BECAUSE the earlier TV meal used slow speed. IF watching TV with TV on the right AND neutral affect AND savory foods with sauce available THEN medium BECAUSE the newer right-TV meal increased speed somewhat while still avoiding the fastest option. IF working on a laptop or tablet with laptop in front AND neutral affect AND savory foods with sauce available THEN slow BECAUSE the new front-laptop meal preserved the comfort-oriented pace even in a work context. IF working on a laptop or tablet in other observed neutral contexts AND any food condition THEN variable, with slow, medium, and fast all observed BECAUSE work contexts sometimes increase efficiency preference but do not override the user's frequent comfort preference.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND solid foods requiring fork acquisition THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE this was selected in earlier observed meals, consistent with prioritizing bite acquisition success. IF social setting with a person on the left AND neutral affect AND nugget-like or solid forkable foods THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the observed social meal used the success-oriented option. IF social setting with a person on the right AND neutral affect AND fruit pieces or other forkable bite-sized items THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the mirrored social fruit meal also used perpendicular skewering, reinforcing this as the general default for reliable acquisition. IF watching TV AND neutral affect AND solid forkable foods THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE observed TV meals used perpendicular skewering. IF working on a laptop or tablet AND neutral affect AND bite-sized sandwich pieces or similar foods where narrower bites may help transfer THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE one observed working meal used parallel skewering, suggesting food geometry can override the general success-oriented default. IF working on a laptop or tablet AND neutral affect AND nugget-like, fish-bite-like, or other solid forkable foods THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE multiple working meals with solid forkable foods, including the new fish-bite meal, used the acquisition-success option.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF working context AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN no BECAUSE the user repeatedly skipped explicit bite-acquisition confirmation to reduce interaction burden. IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND foods without sauces or dips THEN no BECAUSE the user also skipped confirmation in a personal setting. IF social setting with a person on the left AND neutral affect AND savory foods with sauce available THEN no BECAUSE the user declined confirmation in a social meal. IF social setting with a person on the right AND neutral affect AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN no BECAUSE the mirrored social meal preserved the same low-interruption preference. IF watching TV AND neutral affect AND savory foods with sauce available THEN no BECAUSE observed TV meals also skipped confirmation.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user cannot lean forward due to lack of trunk control, making outside-mouth transfer physically unsuitable.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN not applicable BECAUSE the user's physical abilities support inside-mouth transfer rather than outside-mouth transfer.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND foods without sauces or dips THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user selected multimodal signaling in personal dining. IF social setting with a person on the left AND neutral affect AND savory foods with sauce available THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the social meal also used multimodal signaling. IF social setting with a person on the right AND neutral affect AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the mirrored social meal preserved the same preference. IF watching TV AND neutral affect AND savory foods with sauce available THEN speech + LED BECAUSE observed TV meals used multimodal signaling. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN speech + LED BECAUSE observed left-work meals used multimodal signaling. IF working on a laptop or tablet with laptop in front AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the new front-laptop meal also used multimodal signaling. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the right AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN mixed evidence: speech in one meal and speech + LED in another BECAUSE right-side work contexts do not show a stable simplification to audio-only cues.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND foods without sauces or dips THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user chose a low-effort but still user-driven readiness signal in personal dining. IF social setting with a person on the left AND neutral affect AND savory foods with sauce available THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user still preferred user-driven, low-effort initiation in a social meal. IF social setting with a person on the right AND neutral affect AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN open mouth BECAUSE the mirrored social meal again used open-mouth initiation, indicating social context alone does not push this user toward automation. IF watching TV with TV in front AND neutral affect AND savory foods with sauce available THEN open mouth BECAUSE the earlier TV meal also used user-driven open-mouth initiation. IF watching TV with TV on the right AND neutral affect AND savory foods with sauce available THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the newer right-TV meal shifted to more automation while the user was occupied with media. IF working on a laptop or tablet with laptop in front AND neutral affect AND savory foods with sauce available THEN open mouth BECAUSE the new front-laptop meal shows that even while working, the user may retain low-effort explicit control when the screen is directly ahead. IF working on a laptop or tablet in other observed neutral contexts AND any food condition THEN mixed open mouth and autocontinue, with open mouth now observed repeatedly and autocontinue observed at least once BECAUSE work contexts can increase automation preference, but the user often still prefers low-effort explicit control. IF any context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN not button BECAUSE severe upper-limb paralysis prevents reliable button use.

IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND foods without sauces or dips THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user selected the same low-effort, user-driven readiness method for drinking in personal dining. IF social setting with a person on the left AND neutral affect AND savory foods with sauce available THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user also chose open-mouth initiation for drinking in a social meal. IF social setting with a person on the right AND neutral affect AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN open mouth BECAUSE the mirrored social meal again used open-mouth initiation for drinking. IF watching TV with TV in front AND neutral affect AND savory foods with sauce available THEN open mouth BECAUSE the earlier TV meal also used open-mouth initiation for drinking. IF watching TV with TV on the right AND neutral affect AND savory foods with sauce available THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the newer right-TV meal shifted to automated progression while the user was occupied with media. IF working on a laptop or tablet with laptop in front AND neutral affect AND savory foods with sauce available THEN open mouth BECAUSE the new front-laptop meal again favored low-effort explicit control for drinking readiness. IF working on a laptop or tablet in other observed neutral contexts AND any food condition THEN mixed open mouth and autocontinue, with open mouth now observed repeatedly and autocontinue observed at least once BECAUSE work contexts can favor automation, but the user often retains low-effort explicit control. IF any context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN not button BECAUSE severe upper-limb paralysis prevents reliable button use.

IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND foods without sauces or dips THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user accepted automatic initiation for mouth wiping even when choosing more explicit readiness control for feeding and drinking. IF working context AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN autocontinue BECAUSE observed working meals, including the new front-laptop meal, also used automation. IF social setting with a person on the left AND neutral affect AND savory foods with sauce available THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the social meal preserved the same automated wiping pattern. IF social setting with a person on the right AND neutral affect AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the mirrored social meal also preserved the same automated wiping pattern. IF watching TV AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN autocontinue BECAUSE observed TV meals preserved the same automated wiping pattern. IF any context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN not button BECAUSE severe upper-limb paralysis prevents reliable button use.

convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transf.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND foods without sauces or dips THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user selected multimodal completion cues in personal dining. IF social setting with a person on the left AND neutral affect AND savory foods with sauce available THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the social meal also used multimodal completion cues. IF social setting with a person on the right AND neutral affect AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the mirrored social meal preserved the same preference. IF watching TV AND neutral affect AND savory foods with sauce available THEN speech + LED BECAUSE observed TV meals used multimodal completion cues. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN speech + LED BECAUSE observed left-work meals used multimodal completion cues. IF working on a laptop or tablet with laptop in front AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the new front-laptop meal also used multimodal completion cues. detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN perception BECAUSE the user consistently selected sensor-based completion detection, cannot use physical buttons, and this minimizes effort.

detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND foods without sauces or dips THEN perception BECAUSE this is the established low-effort default and the user has good head control. IF social setting with a person on the left AND neutral affect AND savory foods with sauce available THEN perception BECAUSE the user previously selected perception-based drinking completion. IF social setting with a person on the right AND neutral affect AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN perception BECAUSE the mirrored social meal also used perception. IF working on a laptop or tablet AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN perception BECAUSE working meals, including the new front-laptop meal, used perception, reinforcing that work context does not by itself require automated completion. IF watching TV with TV on the right AND neutral affect AND savory foods with sauce available THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the newer right-TV meal shifted to more automation for drinking completion while the user was occupied with media. detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND foods without sauces or dips THEN perception BECAUSE this is the established default in the summary so far and is compatible with the user's good head control. IF working on a laptop or tablet AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN perception BECAUSE working meals, including the new front-laptop meal, used perception, showing that work context can still support explicit sensed completion for wiping. IF watching TV with TV on the right AND neutral affect AND savory foods with sauce available THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the newer right-TV meal used automated completion for wiping. IF any context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN not button BECAUSE severe upper-limb paralysis prevents reliable button use.

retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF working context AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN no BECAUSE repeated working meals, including the new front-laptop meal, preferred to avoid extra motion and reduce meal time even when front occlusion mattered. IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND foods without sauces or dips THEN no BECAUSE the user also skipped retraction in a personal setting. IF social setting with a person on the left AND neutral affect AND savory foods with sauce available THEN no BECAUSE the user still preferred not to retract between bites in a social meal. IF social setting with a person on the right AND neutral affect AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN no BECAUSE the mirrored social meal again favored efficiency over view-clearing despite a nearby partner. IF watching TV with TV in front AND neutral affect AND savory foods with sauce available THEN yes BECAUSE the earlier TV meal accepted extra motion to keep the front view clear. IF watching TV with TV on the right AND neutral affect AND savory foods with sauce available THEN no BECAUSE the newer right-TV meal did not preserve a side view strongly enough to justify retraction.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF foods without sauces or dips AND any context AND any affective state THEN do not dip BECAUSE dipping is not relevant. IF foods include a dip or sauce AND working context AND neutral affect THEN do not dip BECAUSE repeated working meals with available sauces, including the new front-laptop meal, used no dipping, consistent with minimizing mess while working. IF foods include a dip or sauce AND social setting with a person on the left AND neutral affect THEN less BECAUSE the observed social meal used some dipping but not maximal dipping, consistent with wanting sauce while limiting messiness in a social context. IF foods include a dip or sauce AND watching TV AND neutral affect THEN do not dip BECAUSE observed TV meals with available sauces used no dipping.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND foods without sauces or dips THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the user selected a longer wait in personal dining. IF social setting with a person on the left AND neutral affect AND savory foods with sauce available THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the observed social meal also used the longer wait. IF social setting with a person on the right AND neutral affect AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the mirrored social meal again used the longer wait, reinforcing that social dining favors ample response time. IF watching TV AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN 100 sec BECAUSE observed TV meals also used the longer wait. IF working on a laptop or tablet AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN 100 sec with some earlier shorter-wait exceptions BECAUSE the new front-laptop meal adds to the evidence that the user often prefers ample time to intervene even while working.

microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining AND neutral/unspecified affect AND foods without sauces or dips THEN 2 min BECAUSE this remains the repeated reheating choice in personal/default-like contexts. IF watching TV with TV on the right AND neutral/unspecified affect AND solid savory foods, including foods served with sauce on the side such as fish bites with tartar sauce or chicken nuggets with ketchup, THEN either 1 min or 2 min, with 2 min currently more recurrent, BECAUSE both values have been explicitly selected in that occupied TV context.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining AND neutral/unspecified affect AND foods without sauces or dips THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE this has been repeatedly selected in personal contexts. IF watching TV with TV on the right AND neutral/unspecified affect AND any food condition THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE this has now been selected repeatedly in that viewing context, indicating a stable tendency to preserve visibility toward the TV on the right.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining AND neutral/unspecified affect AND foods without sauces or dips THEN medium BECAUSE this is the repeated choice in personal/default-like meals. IF watching TV with TV on the right AND neutral/unspecified affect AND solid savory foods, including foods served with sauce on the side, THEN either medium or fast, with medium now better supported, BECAUSE both have been selected in that occupied context but medium has recurred more often.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF feeding bite-sized sandwich-like foods AND neutral/unspecified affect AND no sauce/dip requirement THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE the user selected this option, consistent with narrower bites that may be easier to eat. IF feeding solid strip-like, chunked, breaded, or nugget-style savory foods such as chicken breast strips, General Tso's chicken with broccoli, breaded fish bites with potatoes, or chicken nuggets with broccoli AND neutral/unspecified affect AND any sauce condition THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected this option for these entrées, consistent with prioritizing bite acquisition success for those food geometries.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining AND neutral/unspecified affect AND foods without sauces or dips THEN no BECAUSE the user repeatedly skipped explicit bite-acquisition confirmation in personal/default-like contexts. IF watching TV AND neutral/unspecified affect AND solid foods, including foods served with sauce on the side, THEN no BECAUSE the user again preferred faster interaction without an extra confirmation step. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user cannot lean forward due to lack of trunk control, making outside-mouth transfer physically unsuitable.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN not applicable BECAUSE the user's physical abilities strongly support inside-mouth transfer rather than outside-mouth transfer.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF watching TV with TV on the right AND neutral/unspecified affect AND any food condition THEN either speech or speech + LED, with speech currently more recurrent, BECAUSE both have been explicitly selected in that context. IF quiet personal dining AND neutral/unspecified affect AND solid savory foods without sauces or dips THEN either speech or speech + LED BECAUSE both have been explicitly selected in personal meals, indicating comfort with auditory-only or multimodal cueing rather than a strict single-cue requirement.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining AND neutral/unspecified affect AND solid savory foods without sauces or dips THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user explicitly selected this low-effort but still user-driven method in a personal context. IF watching TV AND neutral/unspecified affect AND any food condition THEN either open mouth or autocontinue, with autocontinue now better supported, BECAUSE both have been selected while occupied but recent TV meals favor greater automation. IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN button is disfavored by physical constraint BECAUSE the user cannot press physical buttons. Across observed cases, the tie-break pattern is to prefer the least physically demanding workable method, while allowing more automation when attention is occupied.

IF quiet personal dining AND neutral/unspecified affect AND solid savory foods without sauces or dips THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user explicitly selected this method for drinking in a personal context. IF watching TV AND neutral/unspecified affect AND any food condition THEN either open mouth or autocontinue, with autocontinue now better supported, BECAUSE both have been explicitly selected in that occupied context and recent TV evidence favors automation. IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN button is disfavored by physical constraint BECAUSE the user cannot press physical buttons. Across observed cases, the user balances control and effort by using a low-effort non-manual signal when desired and accepting automation in occupied contexts.

detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking.user_tendencies: IF drinking AND quiet personal dining or watching TV AND neutral/unspecified affect AND any food condition THEN either perception or autocontinue BECAUSE both have been explicitly selected across observed contexts, with autocontinue now also observed in the TV context. bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF foods without sauces or dips AND any dining context AND any affective state THEN do not dip BECAUSE dipping is unnecessary. IF observed meals with savory foods where a dip or sauce was available, including fish bites with tartar sauce or chicken nuggets with ketchup, AND neutral/unspecified affect AND watching TV or personal contexts THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user still selected no dipping. occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF dining context involves watching TV or working on a screen in front AND affective state is neutral AND food has no special sauce/dip requirement THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the user explicitly preferred preserving the forward view in a front-facing attention context.
